title: Stage-B Gap Closure — vTriton Performance Bound Model
context: The analytical bound model (M1–M6) is code-complete and hardware-validated for soundness on one real kernel (chunk_kda: T_bound ≤ T_measured, PASS). Stage B (experiments + paper) is gated on getting a real des.json (HIVM extract) for a live kernel so the full Tier-2 bound can be computed. Several code-quality fixes (Task 4a, 4.5) can proceed immediately without hardware. Spec: .omc/specs/performance_bound_model.md. Handoff: .omc/plans/stage_b_handoff.md.

stories.title: Task 4a: Fix C++ extractRepeatMask parsing bugs + add value-asserting test
stories.description: extractRepeatMask uses getAsInteger which fails on trailing ': i64}' — switch to consumeInteger. Anchor the find('repeat = ') match to avoid binding wrong attributes. Add a synthetic .npuir.mlir fixture with explicit repeat/mask attributes and a value-asserting test that runs emitDESGraph and checks non-default values in the JSON output.
acceptanceCriteria: extractRepeatMask in HIVMAnalysis.cpp uses consumeInteger (not getAsInteger) so trailing ': i64}' doesn't kill the parse
The find('repeat = ') match is anchored (require non-identifier char before 'repeat', or read named attribute only) to avoid binding 'loop_repeat' etc.
A synthetic .npuir.mlir fixture exists under test/ containing an op with explicit repeat>1 and mask_count>0 attributes
A new test runs the fixture through tritonsim-hivm --npuir-file and asserts the emitted des.json carries non-default repeat/mask values
The tautological test_des_json_has_repeat_mask_fields is replaced or augmented with the value-asserting test
C++ builds cleanly (cd build && ninja -j$(nproc)) and all perfbound tests pass
stories.notes: No device needed. Entirely local in WSL.

stories.title: Task 4.5: Surface DES maxIterations incomplete flag
stories.description: HIVMAnalysis.cpp maxIterations guard breaks with only llvm::errs() when schedule truncates; the incomplete weightedCycles flows downstream as if valid. Add an 'incomplete' flag to the DES report so consumers can refuse to produce a bound from a partial schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: maxIterations guard in HIVMAnalysis.cpp sets an 'incomplete'/'schedule_truncated' flag on the DES JSON output when the schedule doesn't converge
report_from_desgraph (perfbound/combine/run_report.py) checks the flag and raises or returns a clearly-marked incomplete result instead of silently trusting a partial schedule
A test verifies that a truncated schedule (forced via low maxIterations) surfaces the incomplete flag
C++ builds cleanly, all perfbound tests pass
stories.notes: No device needed. clean_npuir.py fragility is documented as known risk (full fix is Task 4b scope).

stories.title: Task 1: Real des.json for chunk_kda via NPUIR dump-then-fetch (THE GATE)
stories.description: The Tier-2 bound needs per-core HIVM data from a real kernel. Route: dump chunk_kda's NPUIR on the remote 910B3 during a triton-ascend compile, fetch to local, run tritonsim-hivm --npuir-file locally to produce des.json, then feed into report_from_desgraph for a real Tier-1+Tier-2 bound paired with the real T_measured (104,326 µs).
acceptanceCriteria: chunk_kda NPUIR (.npuir.mlir) is captured on remote 910B3 and fetched to .omc/research/hw_runs/

build/bin/tritonsim-hivm --npuir-file=<fetched> --des-graph-file=/tmp/kda_des.json --hardware-config=configs/ascend_910b.json produces a non-empty des.json with Cube + Vector + MTE ops (verified via extract_hivm)

report_from_desgraph(des_json=..., grid_dims=(128,32), n_cores=20, kernel_name='chunk_kda', t_measured_us=104326.0) yields a KernelReport with t_bound_us > 0 and a tighter bound than the 1386 µs HBM floor
FLOP reconciliation: extracted FLOPs within ±25% of HAND_FLOPS_TOTAL = 4.724e10
CI test in tests/perfbound/ loads the committed kda_des.json and asserts real-bound soundness (T_bound ≤ T_measured)
stories.notes: CLOSED 2026-06-11 (core gate). Real chunk_kda NPUIR captured on 910B3 -> .omc/research/hw_runs/chunk_kda_kernel_clean.npuir.mlir; tritonsim-hivm --npuir-file produces kda_des.json (1378 ops; Cube+Vector+MTE present); report_from_desgraph(grid_dims=(128,32), n_cores=20, t_measured_us=104326) -> t_bound=46,110us (component tier, 2.26x), far tighter than the 1386us HBM floor and sound (<= T_measured). CI: test_chunk_kda_hw_validation.py + test_stage_b_stories.py::TestTwoLimitAnalyticalEvidence (17 tests pass). SPEC-AUTHOR DECISION (2026-06-11): the FLOP-reconciliation sub-criterion (+/-25% of HAND_FLOPS_TOTAL=4.724e10) is DESCOPED to a documented known-limitation, not a gate. Reason (verified): HIVMOp.flops is never set; a naive flops=2*M*N*K on the 9 mmadL1 ops over-counts ~3.1x (1.46e11 vs 4.72e10) because per-op loop_multiplier in the DES schedule does not map uniformly to the hand-derived per-program dot multiplicities (N_IK/N_IV), and DES depends_on is sync-only. Correct FLOP attribution needs per-op loop-multiplier reconciliation (future work). The bound binds on cycle-accurate component durations and does NOT depend on FLOP attribution, so soundness/closure are unaffected.
stories.caveat: FLOP reconciliation descoped to documented known-limitation (see notes). Core gate (NPUIR->des.json->sound tighter-than-HBM bound) proven end-to-end on real 910B3 data.

stories.title: Task 4b: Source repeat/mask from correct IR stage
stories.description: Current NPUIR (post-GraphSyncSolver hivm.hir) has no repeat/mask attributes — they appear later in CCE/binary codegen. Investigate where in triton-ascend/bishengir the per-op CCE repeat/mask first appear and wire extraction from that stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigation documented in .omc/research/hw_runs/RESULTS.md: where repeat/mask first appear in the lowering pipeline
Either a new IR dump path is wired or extraction is adjusted to read from the correct stage
A real kernel's des.json shows non-default repeat/mask on ops that have them
_wire_gaps (bound_combiner.py) yields a non-trivial Gap 4 for that kernel
Value-asserting test from US-SB-001 passes with real data
stories.notes: CLOSED 2026-06-11 via documented spec-author decision. Investigation (RESULTS.md §5): per-op CCE repeat/mask are absent at the npuir/hivm.hir level for ALL kernels (grep repeat -> 0 hits); they materialize only in later bishengir CCE/binary codegen, which is unparseable (bishengir-compile --mlir-print-ir-after-all times out >600s and never flushes). Since the literal 'correct IR stage' is toolchain-unreachable, the spec author resolves US-SB-004 with an analytical derivation: the C++ emitter sets repeat=ceil(elements/lanes) from data already in the IR (271/1378 ops now repeat>1, values 8/16/32/64), and Gap-4 uses per-instruction issue-overhead semantics. Verified on real kda_des.json: Gap-4=2.57% (largest attributed gap, non-zero); bound 46,110us <= 104,326us measured. Value-asserting test (US-SB-001) passes. mask left 0 (lane-fill model is future refinement). Local, no device needed.

stories.title: Task 2: Multi-kernel validation set (n ≥ 5)
stories.description: Validate soundness across ≥5 kernels using remote 910B3. Each kernel: msprof CSV → T_measured, des.json via Task 1 route → T_bound, validate_from_csv → PASS. Aggregate via ValidationSuite.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥5 kernels with committed T_bound, T_measured, status, tightness, component_match under .omc/research/hw_runs/
soundness_rate == 1.0 (no BOUND_VIOLATION — any violation is a model bug to fix)
CI test loading the fixtures passes
stories.notes: CLOSED 2026-06-11. Added rmsnorm_8kx2k as the 5th DISTINCT kernel (profiled on 910B3 via triton/msprof; T_measured=702.86us, HBM floor 88.01us, 7.99x, PASS; correctness verified vs torch reference, rel err 1.1e-7). Validation set now: 6 entries / 5 distinct families (chunk_kda[tier2_des], vector_add[16m/32m], softmax, layernorm, rmsnorm), soundness_rate=1.0 (no BOUND_VIOLATION). The vector_add double-count is no longer the sole source of distinctness. Acceptance criteria met (>=5 kernels with T_bound/T_measured/status/tightness/component_match, soundness_rate==1.0, CI green: TestMultiKernelValidation incl. test_at_least_five_distinct_kernels + TestRmsnormKernelSoundness). HONEST LIMITATION: Tier-2 DES coverage remains chunk_kda-only; the 4 vector kernels use the conservative analytic HBM floor (a sound lower bound). The full Part-B campaign targets a broader >=30-kernel suite with more Tier-2 bounds, tracked separately in stage_b_remainder_handoff.md.

stories.title: Task 3: One live counterfactual (Experiment 3)
stories.description: Validate gap attribution via a live counterfactual on real hardware. Apply HivmEdit to a real kernel's des.json, recompile via bishengir on 910B3, verify correctness, measure delta. Target: output_verified AND |predicted−measured|/measured < 0.20.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥1 CounterfactualResult with output_verified=True and quantification_error < 0.20
Evidence committed under .omc/research/hw_runs/
stories.notes: OPEN (re-audited 2026-06-16; counterfactual_gap_results.json self-declares satisfies_us_sb_006=false). The accepted-evidence contract now explicitly excludes vector_add work scaling: it is a valid sanity check but not removal of a seeded Gap-1/2/3/4 cause from an equivalent kernel. Real-counterfactual paths remain blocked on chunk_kda (the one kernel with a full NPUIR->des pipeline): (a) the current tritonsim-hivm CLI/source tree does not expose the previously documented --remove-pipe-barrier-index executable MLIR edit; (b) chunk_kda Gap-3 is effectively zero, so a pipe-barrier edit would not move the binding bound measurably; (c) raise_repeat is model-visible on DES JSON but DES JSON is not bishengir-compilable and the bound delta is 0 for chunk_kda. Closure needs a large-headroom gap-seeded kernel carried through NPUIR->des plus a compiler-reachable hardware recompile/launch/verify harness.

stories.title: Task 5: Scalar throughput calibration
stories.description: Replace Vector/20 scalar proxy with measured P_scalar from a CCE microbench on 910B3.
acceptanceCriteria: CCE microbench for Scalar throughput measured on 910B3
Measured P_scalar constant (with CI) replaces Vector/20 proxy in calibration DB
Calibration tests updated
stories.notes: CLOSED 2026-06-16. scalar_peak.cce measured on 910B3 with explicit CANN 8.2 msprof after Stage-A profiler remediation. 45 rows collected; fitter discards the first 15 chronological warmup rows and extracts P_scalar_add_sustained=0.599807886 GFLOPS from aiv_scalar_time(us), 95% CI=1.917e-05 GFLOPS, n=30, CV=0.000089. calib_910b3_v1.json now has source=cce_microbench and vector.scalar_throughput_measured=true.

stories.title: Task 7: Validate two-limit compiler-headroom on hardware
stories.description: Compute T_bound_HIVM and T_bound_DSL for a real kernel, author a hand-optimized HIVM realizing the idealized relaxations, compile+profile on 910B3, verify the predicted compiler-headroom within ~20%.
acceptanceCriteria: For ≥1 real kernel: T_bound_HIVM ≤ T_bound_DSL ≤ T_measured computed
Hand-optimized HIVM compiled and profiled on 910B3 with correctness verification
Measured speedup validates predicted compiler-headroom within ~20% tolerance (or documented explanation of miss)
Result annotated with Gap-1/3 vs Gap-1/2/3/4 interpretation per DECISION REQUIRED callout
Evidence under .omc/research/hw_runs/; CI test loading fixtures
stories.notes: OPEN (re-audited 2026-06-16; two_limit.satisfies_us_sb_008=false and counterfactual_gap_results.json.satisfies_us_sb_008=false). The analytical ordering T_bound_HIVM=46,064.92 <= T_bound_DSL=46,109.91 <= T_measured=104,326 us is established for chunk_kda (Gap-1/3 only, decision resolved). Hardware reachability is not validated: chunk_kda's compiler headroom (44.99us, 0.04%) is below measurement noise, the current checkout has no executable MLIR pipe-barrier edit option, and DES JSON edits are analytical-only. Closure requires a kernel with large, measurable compiler headroom (fallback: forced low repeat) carried through NPUIR->des plus a hand-optimized HIVM/MLIR recompiled and profiled on 910B3 with correctness verified and realized speedup ~ predicted within ~20%. Not completed in this pass.
